header.stance: 能源與工廠價格壓力在紐約收盤時推動由政策預期主導的美國公債熊市趨平

zh-TW.long_thesis_markdown:
## 固定收益開場論點

前一收盤日短暫出現的能源壓力緩解於 June 1 逆轉，美國公債透過政策敏感的短端對此重新定價，而非再次遭遇長端期限溢價衝擊。2 年期殖利率上升 7 個基點至 4.05%，10 年期則僅上升 2 個基點至 4.47%。此一分歧使該交易日成為因通膨與 Federal Reserve 風險再起而形成的熊市趨平，而非普遍撤離存續期間。

最強的傳導管道來自美伊談判的不確定性再起。有報導稱德黑蘭已暫停與華盛頓交換訊息，推動原油大幅上漲；其後雖有會談持續的保證，債券卻僅部分回升。WTI 收盤仍報 $92.16，上漲 5.4945%。油價走勢重新開啟前一收盤日一度減弱的通膨傳導風險，推升通膨補償，並恢復供給中斷可能使政策維持緊縮的風險，即使基礎需求最終放緩亦然。

美國製造業隨後進一步強化短端壓力。ISM 製造業指數升至 54.0，高於 53.2 的市場共識，訂單與生產均轉強，價格分項則處於異常高檔。另一項工廠調查也顯示產出加快、成本上升及交貨嚴重延誤。此一組合挑戰了現行政策立場已在明確冷卻經濟活動的看法。交易員將 Fed 下次升息的預期時點提前，使短天期及中期債券比長債承受更大風險。

抵銷力量來自民間部門持續的風險承接。股票上漲，大型收購與 AI 相關融資進入市場，一家近期遭降評的商業發展公司更將債券發行規模擴大至原先的逾 2 倍。信用總報酬指標下跌，但跌勢伴隨政府債券殖利率上升，而非融資管道崩潰。因此，收盤傳遞出帶有風險韌性的停滯性通膨政策訊息：能源與工廠定價重新開啟緊縮風險，但初級市場運作順暢且成長股堅挺，使走勢未演變為防禦性拋售。

## 美國公債利率、Fed 定價、實質殖利率與殖利率曲線結構

美國公債跌幅隨到期年限拉長而穩定收斂。5 年期殖利率上升 5 個基點至 4.18%，10 年期上升 2 個基點至 4.47%，30 年期則持平於 4.99%。2 年期上升 7 個基點，使 2s10s 趨平 5 個基點至 42 個基點，2s30s 趨平 7 個基點至 94。殖利率曲線將衝擊定位於預期政策，以及中段對通膨持續性再起的曝險；遠端在先前實質殖利率調整後則相對穩定。

盤中走勢支持此一解讀。美伊暫停交換訊息的報導推動美國原油大幅上漲時，各期限殖利率一度上升多達 5 至 9 個基點。華盛頓表示以色列與 Hezbollah 已同意停止攻擊，且與伊朗的談判仍在繼續後，美國公債收復部分跌幅。反彈不完整，使收盤價明確保留地緣政治溢價；但長債最終持平，顯示投資人並未單純將相同的能源溢價加諸每個期限。

通膨分解顯示變動並未集中於長天期實質利率。10 年期 TIPS 殖利率持平於 2.07%，10 年期損益兩平通膨率則上升 2 個基點至 2.4%，解釋了名目 10 年期殖利率的全部漲幅。5 年期及 7 年期實質殖利率分別上升 3 及 2 個基點至 1.64% 與 1.85%。20 年期及 30 年期實質殖利率持平於 2.49% 與 2.71%。較短期限同時吸收更緊縮的實質政策路徑及能源通膨，長端則未新增任何實質利率讓利。

殖利率曲線曲率也反映相同的期限分化。2s5s10s 蝶式利差上升 1 個基點至負 16，顯示 5 年期區段相對兩翼小幅落後。2s10s30s 蝶式利差下跌 3 個基點至負 10，因 10 年期相對持平的長債變得便宜，但幅度小於 2 年期。這些是受控的相對價值變動，而非失序證據。曲線中段仍是升息時點、中期通膨補償及能源衝擊將持續多久之不確定性的交會點。

製造業數據構成此輪賣壓的國內因素。ISM 新訂單由 54.1 升至 56.8，生產由 53.4 升至 54.3。就業由 46.4 改善至 48.6，但仍處於萎縮區間；供應商交貨維持在 60.6，價格則僅由 84.6 降至 82.1。美國製造業 PMI 終值由 54.5 升至 55.1，產出成長創 April 2022 以來最快，成本壓力再起、庫存增加，出口則連續第 11 個月疲弱。對利率市場而言，強勁活動與供應摩擦壓過了偏弱的勞動細節。

營建支出對成長訊號有所保留，但未使其逆轉。April 支出增加 0.4%，年率達 $2.1724 trillion，高於公布前 0.1% 的預測，但官方信賴區間為正負 0.7 個百分點。私人住宅營建增加 0.8%，私人非住宅支出則下跌 0.2%，製造業營建支出單月下降 1.2%，較一年前下降 18.5%。對利率敏感的住宅市場維持動能，但企業固定投資仍不均衡；該數據更支持經濟具韌性，而非全面加速。

交換利率定價將這些因素轉化為更迫近的緊縮路徑。市場已完全定價 March 2027 前升息，並認為最早於 October 行動的機率約為 50%。這項重新定價解釋了為何 2 年期及 5 年期跌幅最大，即使長天期實質殖利率維持穩定。市場並非宣告決策迫在眉睫，而是在油價、工廠需求及供應鏈價格均朝相反方向施壓時，撤除耐心等待最終必然導向寬鬆的信心。

政策敏感期限的國庫券殖利率也走高。13 週及 26 週等值票息殖利率各上升 3 個基點，分別至 3.71% 與 3.78%；4 週及 8 週期限則上升 1 個基點。美國財政部以 3.630% 的最高貼現率標售 $89 billion 的 91 天期國庫券，投標倍數為 2.69；另以 3.665% 標售 $77 billion 的 182 天期國庫券，投標倍數為 2.84。這些結果確立短端供給已完成承接；其後的交割仍是獨立的現金事件，而非 June 1 收盤走勢的解釋。

## 信用、融資與初級市場承接

即使利率衝擊拖累掛牌信用商品報酬，公司融資管道仍保持開放。LQD 下跌 0.3932%，HYG 下跌 0.5852%，JNK 下跌 0.62%。這些工具結合美國公債存續期間、票息收益及信用風險，因此較為繁忙的初級市場更能清楚反映融資可得性。高收益借款人合計發行 $5.5 billion 債券，為 5 週來最繁忙的交易日，顯示賣壓雖提高折現率門檻，卻未使市場關閉。

FS KKR Capital 提供最明確的已完成承接案例。這家商業發展公司原先目標為至少 $400 million，最終發行 $900 million 的垃圾債券。債券殖利率為 7.5%，位於初步討論之 7% 中段至高段區間的較低端，並吸引 $1.5 billion 需求。這項執行格外值得注意，因 Moody’s 與 Fitch 此前因貸款減記及削減股利，已將該公司降至垃圾級；KKR 近期也提供 $300 million 特別股支援及 $150 million 股份收購要約。

此交易顯示，只要定價與贊助方支援足以補償風險，投資人願意向面臨挑戰的私募信貸工具提供資金。但這並未消除其基礎貸款組合的惡化。募集資金可用於債務再融資，而更廣泛的機構背景也具建設性：商業發展公司年內已籌資超過 $14 billion，自該市場區段於 April 重新開放以來，發行量超過 $5.5 billion。因此，融資可得性取決於明確的安全邊際，而非市場不加區別地追逐次順位或不透明的信用曝險。

QXO Building Products 帶來規模更大的收購融資考驗。銀行開始推銷 $3 billion 垃圾債券，以取代其收購 TopBuild 所使用的過橋融資，另搭配一筆 $3 billion 定期貸款。債券部分包含兩批各 $1.5 billion、分別於 2031 及 2034 到期的券種。初期貸款討論條件約為基準利率加 225 個基點，發行價 99 至 99.5，用以支應一項約 $17 billion 收購案的部分資金。稀缺的 M&A 券種可能吸引需求，但此方案也將建材產品的週期性及執行風險集中於槓桿型資本結構。

AI 基礎設施延伸了供給管道。Elk Grove Village Property 尋求發行 $850 million 垃圾債券，為芝加哥地區一座資料中心提供資金；該中心已全數出租予 CoreWeave，租期 15 年，代表約 $2.2 billion 收入。募集資金將用於再融資 $500 million 的現有債務與利息，並支應剩餘工程。與 AI 建設相關的公司年內已發行近 $27 billion 的高風險債券。長期合約收入提高可預見性，但工程完工、租戶集中度與再融資仍將決定投資人要求多少利差。

貸款市場則透過重新定價與延長到期日展現需求。包括 Restaurant Brands Europe、nexeye 及 Parts Holding Europe 在內的歐洲借款人，尋求將現有歐元貸款利差下調 25 至 50 個基點；年初至今發行量較前一年落後約 8%，折合 $124.8 billion。Citadel Securities 推銷一筆 2033 到期的 $4.25 billion 貸款，初始利差為 200 個基點、發行價 99.5 至 99.75，藉此延長一筆 2031 到期的 $3.95 billion 融資並新增 $300 million。供給稀缺支持貸款人延續曝險的意願，但反覆壓縮利差也降低了其補償。

Atalian 展現信用分化的另一端。持有這家法國設施管理集團約 60%、總額 €919 million 且於 2028 到期之債券的投資人，同意透過重組取得控制權。約三分之二的負債將轉換為多數股權，其餘債務延長至 2031，投資人並承諾提供最多 €140 million 的新超優先順位融資。因此，初級市場管道雖廣泛，卻非人人可得：體質健全或獲充分支援的借款人可以再融資並擴大規模，資產負債表受損者則需要股權化、延長到期日及新增優先順位資金。

## 全球存續期間、央行與財政傳導

歐洲利率為美國熊市趨平提供了鷹派的全球背景。ECB 執行委員 Isabel Schnabel 主張，伊朗戰爭衝擊已不能再被視為暫時性的能源擾動，因為基礎設施與供應鏈受損，正將壓力擴散至商品價格與預期。她支持升息，但也對首次行動後的政策路徑保持審慎。此一立場推升全球短端折現率；在美國工廠價格同樣高漲之際，也使美國公債更難維持獨立的寬鬆敘事。

歐元區製造業使政策選擇更為複雜。May PMI 終值由 52.2 降至 51.6，產出指數則由 52.3 放緩至 51.3。新訂單停滯、出口下降且就業萎縮，但投入成本增速創 May 2022 以來最快，產出收費通膨則升至 3 年半高點。活動疲弱通常應支持存續期間，但供應鏈通膨加速卻指向更緊縮的政策。由此形成的停滯性通膨組合，透過相對政策定價及全球期限溢價競爭，將波動傳導至美國利率。

消費者預期對最鷹派的解讀提供一些制衡。歐元區 3 年期通膨預期於 April 由 3% 降至 2.9%，1 年期預期維持 4%，5 年期指標持平於 2.4%，仍高於 ECB 目標。中期預期的小幅改善並未消除短期及長期疑慮持續存在的情況，反而使決策者必須在製造業放緩與能源及供應鏈成本可能固化的風險之間取得平衡，這與 Fed 面臨的權衡相似。

日本增添了匯率及投資人基礎的傳導管道。創紀錄的干預仍未克服美日之間寬廣的利差，隔夜交換利率顯示 BOJ 近期升息的機率約為 78%。高油價增加日本進口帳單及通膨壓力，日圓疲弱則降低干預成果的持久性。更鷹派的 BOJ 可能推升國內殖利率，並降低避險後外國債券的相對吸引力，使美國及歐洲存續期間必須在較不可靠的日本需求下完成去化。

南韓透過供給管理來控制相同的壓力。自 May 18 起，財政部官員每日與交易商及資產管理公司通話；政府並在 10 年期殖利率升至 late 2023 以來最高水準後，將原訂 June 債券發行量削減約 21%。供給下降可緩和失序上漲，卻無法消除輸入性通膨或央行轉趨鷹派的立場。因此，政策回應僅在邊際上緩解當地存續期間壓力，並未改變全球能源傳導機制。

在紐約截止時間之後，南韓 May 消費者通膨由 2.6% 加速至 3.1%，高於 2.9% 的預估中位數，核心通膨則達 2.5%。該數據並未造成已完成的美國公債走勢，卻強化了下一交易日的風險：燃料及旅遊成本正由表面能源向外擴散，支持 Bank of Korea 的鷹派傾向。中國在較早時段提供抵銷力量：製造業 PMI 因出口轉弱而由 52.2 降至 51.8，投入及產出價格通膨也放緩。

## 新興市場主權債、美元流動性與債務管理風險

新興市場美元債面臨較不利的美國利率、原油及美元組合。EMB 下跌 0.4355%，美元指數上升 0.2932%；高油價增加能源依賴型主權國的進口成本之際，整體外部環境也同步收緊。掛牌基金走勢反映的是總報酬壓力，而非現金利差全面一致變動。各國結果取決於能源帳單如何與外匯存底、財政補貼、當地政策公信力及債務條款相互作用。

印度最接近這一不利交會點。自伊朗戰爭開始以來，其 10 年期主權債殖利率已上升約 34 個基點至 7%，5 年期債券漲幅更大，因市場定價貨幣緊縮。龐大的石油進口、盧比走弱，以及與燃料和肥料相關的補貼，將外部衝擊直接連結至通膨與政府借款。Kotak Mahindra 估計，收入短缺與補貼增加可能使財政赤字由 4.3% 的預算目標擴大至 GDP 的 4.6%，更不利情境下則可能達 4.8%。

印度殖利率曲線定價的幅度已超越單純的輸入性價格飆升。部分經理人預期本輪週期將升息 75 至 100 個基點，5 年期交換利率自衝突開始以來則已上升逾 60 個基點。其他投資人主張，供給主導的通膨應使央行對在需求轉弱時緊縮保持審慎。此一分歧使短期及中期主權債同時對油價持續性與盧比干預敏感，而財政管道則提高政府借款計畫較後段所需的補償。

智利顯示，同一地緣政治因素可以多快逆轉當地相對價值。先前對達成協議的樂觀預期，曾使長期損益兩平通膨率與主權風險指標回落至接近戰前水準，並將投資人興趣轉向短天期名目披索債券。偏好 1 至 5 年期抗通膨工具的比例，由一個月前的 72% 降至接近 54%。其後暫停談判的消息及油價反彈挑戰了此一正常化走勢，再度引發加油站價格上漲會多快傳導至消費者通膨的問題。

尚比亞的爭議源自合約設計，而非廣泛的新興市場 beta。持有其 $1.36 billion、2053 到期美元債券逾 25% 的投資人，反對一項他們認為不利的收購要約。政府對提前參與者每 $1,000 出價 $780，較晚參與者則為 $740；若接受比例達 75%，即可啟動清理買權，在票息可能由 0.5% 上調至 7.5% 前移除剩餘投資人。即使整體美元環境可控，這項衝突仍將風險集中於集體行動、回收價值及債務可持續性條件。

債務交換為面臨高殖利率的開發中主權國提供較緩慢的再融資管道。Enosis 尋求擴大以更佳條件再融資政府債務，並將節省資金導向環境或社會計畫的交易，獲得 Legal & General 提供 $1 billion、為期 5 年的承諾支持。可能符合資格的債務超過 $1 trillion，但自 2021 以來，私募資本交易僅完成約 $6 billion。此一落差同時顯示，在傳統再融資變得昂貴時，結構化債務管理的潛在紓困效果及執行限制。

## 跨資產

原油為利率論點提供最明確的確認。談判停滯的消息再次引發市場對中東供給的擔憂，WTI 上漲 5.4945% 至 $92.16，Brent 上漲 3.1831% 至 $94.98。WTI 較大的漲幅，與 10 年期損益兩平通膨率上升 2 個基點及短端賣壓一致。其後外交保證降低了盤中極端波動，卻未恢復前一收盤日能源壓力緩解的格局，使石油成為該交易日最主要的通膨傳導管道。

股票抵抗了債券市場的緊縮。S&P 500 上漲 0.2625%，Nasdaq 100 上漲 0.5957%，半導體指數上漲 1.0622%。即使較高的實質利率對短天期美國公債形成壓力，AI 獲利與投資預期仍支撐成長股。兩者並存符合名目經濟活動具韌性，而非市場擔憂衰退的情境。這也形成固定收益市場的抵銷力量：大型科技公司的計畫資本支出最高可達 $725 billion，長期可能支持生產力，但初期會增加對晶片、電力、建設及債務融資的需求。

波動率與美元顯示的憂慮程度高於股票。VIX 上漲 4.765% 至 16.05，美元指數上漲 0.2932% 至 99.2，較高的美國殖利率與地緣政治需求彼此強化。槓桿基金、資產管理公司及其他投機帳戶持有約 $16.5 billion 的美元多頭部位，高於 3 週前約 $5 billion。美元走強會加劇進口依賴型主權國所受的石油衝擊，並收緊美國以外地區的融資環境。

儘管地緣政治緊張再起，黃金仍下跌 1.8704% 至 4475.2002。黃金下跌並伴隨股票上漲，顯示該交易日並非一致性的避險潮。投資人獎勵 AI 相關成長題材、上調傳統美元與政策利率曝險的定價，並同步壓低政府債券與黃金價格。信用指標弱於股票，使一項問題仍未解決：若原油維持高檔，充沛的初級市場資本能否繼續抵銷整體資金成本上升。

## 前瞻市場意義

June 2 的 JOLTS、June 3 的 ISM 服務業調查、June 4 的初領失業救濟金人數與修正後生產力數據，以及 June 5 的就業報告，構成短端緊縮重新定價的下一項考驗。可觀察的觸發因素包括職缺、聘僱與離職是否維持強勁；服務業就業及價格是否呼應工廠調查；以及非農就業、失業率、薪資與單位勞動成本是否顯示需求壓力持續。若勞動需求具韌性且服務業價格維持高檔，將確認 June 1 的熊市趨平，並使 2 年期及 5 年期區段持續對 Fed 更早升息保持敏感。若聘僱與價格全面降溫，將挑戰由製造業主導的解讀、降低實質政策壓力，並化解工廠就業萎縮與產出轉強之間的部分矛盾。

美伊談判與荷莫茲海峽航運的狀況，隨後是 June 5 的 RBI 決策、June 10-11 的 ECB 會議及 June 16 的 BOJ 決策，將檢驗能源衝擊是否正成為國際同步的政策動能。可觀察的觸發因素包括石油出口能否持久恢復、原油是否持續下跌、當地貨幣是否走強，以及央行指引能否區分暫時性能源效應與更廣泛的通膨。若航運安全且政策指引保持耐心，將挑戰目前損益兩平通膨率上升的走勢，並重新開啟先前能源壓力緩解的解讀。若干擾持續、原油再度上漲或各央行同步釋出鷹派訊號，則將確認停滯性通膨管道，其中美國損益兩平通膨率、歐洲與亞洲短端、美元及石油進口型主權國殖利率曲線最為敏感。

美國財政部 June 3 的長存續期間債券買回、June 4 的短端現金管理債券買回與附息債券供給公告，以及 June 2-3 期間 QXO 貸款與債券方案的執行，將檢驗金融中介體系能否在較高利率結構下承接公私部門供給。若國庫券殖利率穩定、交割有序、QXO 訂單強勁且定價接近初始討論條件，將確認 June 1 的賣壓反映總體重新定價，而非融資限制。若市場反應不佳、公司債讓利幅度較大，或在已公告的 3 年期、10 年期及 30 年期供給前後長債再度表現落後，則將挑戰此一結論。敏感管道包括附買回與國庫券利率、美國公債曲線中段與長端、高收益債新券讓利，以及更廣泛的再融資管線。
zh-TW.methodology_note: 除非另有註明，市場水準均以紐約收盤為準。

overall_summary.headline: 油價與工廠價格重燃 Fed 緊縮風險，美國公債呈熊市趨平
overall_summary.session_narrative: 美伊不確定性再起推升原油，加上製造業強勁，重新帶回通膨與 Fed 緊縮風險，紐約收盤呈現受控的熊市趨平。衝擊傳導至政策敏感及中期美國公債，但長債與長天期實質殖利率相對穩定。融資市場維持開放且成長股上漲，使此波走勢未演變為全面逃離存續期間或風險資產。
overall_summary.summary: 石油供應疑慮再起，加上美國製造業表現穩健，重新開啟通膨與 Fed 風險，短端美國公債殖利率領漲。長天期實質殖利率維持穩定，初級市場運作順暢且成長股走強，使此波走勢未演變為廣泛的防禦性拋售。

section_summaries.market_read: 收盤走勢是短端政策重新定價且市場風險承接仍具韌性，而非存續期間衝擊。
section_summaries.next_check: 油價漲勢能否持續及即將公布的勞動數據，將決定殖利率曲線趨平能否維持。
section_summaries.summary: 美伊不確定性再起推升原油，重燃通膨傳導風險，而穩健的製造業則加深美國公債短端跌勢。由此形成的熊市趨平反映 Fed 政策路徑更趨緊縮，而非廣泛撤離存續期間；初級市場保持開放且成長股上漲，抑制了防禦性壓力。

section_summaries.market_read: 利率市場將衝擊定位於 Fed 行動時點與中期通膨，期限溢價仍受控制。
section_summaries.next_check: 服務業價格與勞動市場韌性，是短端緊縮預期的下一項考驗。
section_summaries.section_title: 美國公債利率、Fed 定價、實質殖利率與殖利率曲線結構
section_summaries.summary: 美國公債跌幅隨到期年限拉長而收斂，2 年期及 5 年期區段吸收較高的政策與通膨風險，長債與長天期實質殖利率則維持穩定。損益兩平通膨率上升解釋了名目 10 年期殖利率的變動，而蝶式利差僅小幅移動，顯示重新定價受控而非失序。

section_summaries.market_read: 初級市場執行情況顯示折現率門檻升高，而非系統性融資中斷。
section_summaries.next_check: QXO 的訂單品質與讓利幅度，將顯示槓桿型供給能否順利去化。
section_summaries.section_title: 信用、融資與初級市場承接
section_summaries.summary: 信用報酬指標隨美國公債殖利率上升而轉弱，但高收益債供給獲得承接，且一家遭降評的借款人以有利條件擴大發行規模，顯示融資管道仍然暢通。個別差異依然重要：有支援的發行人能夠再融資，資產負債表受損者則需要股權化、延長到期日及優先順位資本。

section_summaries.market_read: 全球政策重新定價，壓縮了美國獨自形成寬鬆敘事的空間。
section_summaries.next_check: ECB、BOJ 與 RBI 的指引必須區分暫時性能源壓力與持續性通膨。
section_summaries.section_title: 全球存續期間、央行與財政傳導
section_summaries.summary: 歐洲供應鏈通膨、央行鷹派訊號及日本投資人基礎的動態，強化了美國公債面臨的全球短端壓力。歐元區活動放緩及韓國對債券供給的管理緩和了存續期間壓力，但兩者都未消除能源衝擊帶來的通膨或跨市場政策傳導。

section_summaries.market_read: 新興市場壓力有所分化，但石油進口型主權國面臨最緊縮的外部環境組合。
section_summaries.next_check: 關注原油漲勢能否持續、當地貨幣穩定性，以及主要石油進口國的政策回應。
section_summaries.section_title: 新興市場主權債、美元流動性與債務管理風險
section_summaries.summary: 美國利率、原油及美元上升，收緊了新興市場債務的金融環境；石油進口國透過通膨、匯率與財政補貼管道承受風險。印度面臨最明顯的政策衝突，而尚比亞的收購要約爭議及緩慢的債務交換執行，凸顯個別再融資與合約風險。

section_summaries.market_read: 跨資產定價反映名目成長具韌性，同時通膨風險再起。
section_summaries.next_check: 若原油持續上漲且風險資產轉弱，將顯示傳導機制更偏防禦性。
section_summaries.summary: 油價確認通膨動能，AI 相關成長題材則推動股票上漲；同時，波動率上升與美元走強顯示整體市場仍較審慎。黃金與債券同步下跌，顯示並非一致性的避險行情，因此信用市場的承接能力成為抵銷融資成本上升的關鍵力量。

section_summaries.market_read: 要確認此一論點，需要需求與通膨持續存在，同時市場承接能力不惡化。
section_summaries.next_check: 追蹤截至 June 16 的三項既定考驗，觀察訊號是否趨於一致或論點明確遭到推翻。
section_summaries.summary: 三項考驗將決定本輪賣壓維持在總體重新定價，或演變為更廣泛的融資問題：美國勞動與服務業數據、能源流動與央行指引，以及美國公債與公司債供給的承接情況。各項管道分別直接牽動短端政策定價、全球通膨傳導或金融中介能力。

watch_events.market_implication: 若獲確認，2 年期及 5 年期美國公債仍將面臨進一步政策重新定價的風險。
watch_events.market_trigger: 若聘僱穩健且服務業價格維持高檔，將驗證 Fed 更早升息的可能性。
watch_events.title: 美國勞動與服務業考驗
watch_events.watch_line: 追蹤職缺、服務業價格、初領失業救濟金人數、非農就業、薪資及單位勞動成本。

watch_events.market_implication: 損益兩平通膨率、全球短端利率、美元及石油進口國殖利率曲線將再度承壓。
watch_events.market_trigger: 若干擾持續、原油再度上漲或各央行同步釋出鷹派指引，將確認壓力具有持續性。
watch_events.title: 能源流動與央行回應
watch_events.watch_line: 關注美伊談判、海峽航運、原油、匯率，以及 RBI、ECB 與 BOJ 的指引。

watch_events.market_implication: 若市場反應不佳或讓利幅度擴大，將對美國公債存續期間及再融資管線形成壓力。
watch_events.market_trigger: 若交割有序且接近初始條件的需求強勁，將確認金融中介功能仍然完整。
watch_events.title: 公私部門供給承接
watch_events.watch_line: 追蹤美國財政部買回與附息債券計畫，以及 QXO 融資方案的執行情況。

long_thesis.english_markdown:
## Opening Fixed-Income Thesis

The previous close’s tentative energy relief reversed on June 1, and Treasuries repriced the reversal through the policy-sensitive front end rather than through a fresh long-end term-premium shock. The two-year yield rose seven basis points to 4.05%, whereas the 10-year increased only two basis points to 4.47%. That divergence made the session a bear-flattening response to renewed inflation and Federal Reserve risk, not a generalized withdrawal from duration.

The strongest transmission channel was the renewed uncertainty around US-Iran negotiations. Reports that Tehran had suspended message exchanges with Washington pushed crude sharply higher before later assurances that talks continued produced only a partial recovery in bonds. WTI still closed at $92.16, up 5.4945%. The oil move reopened the pass-through risk that had eased at the prior close, lifted inflation compensation and restored the possibility that supply disruption could keep policy restrictive even if underlying demand eventually slows.

US manufacturing then reinforced the front-end pressure. The ISM manufacturing index rose to 54.0 against a 53.2 consensus, with stronger orders and production and an exceptionally elevated prices component. A separate factory survey also showed faster output, rising costs and severe delivery delays. The combination challenged the idea that the current policy setting was already cooling activity decisively. Traders brought forward expectations for the Fed’s next increase, leaving shorter and intermediate maturities more exposed than the long bond.

The counterforce was continued private-sector risk absorption. Equities advanced, large acquisition and AI-linked financings entered the market, and a recently downgraded business-development company more than doubled its bond sale. Credit total-return gauges fell, but their losses occurred alongside higher government yields rather than a collapse in financing access. The close therefore carried a stagflationary policy message with a resilient-risk overlay: energy and factory pricing reopened tightening risk, yet functioning primary markets and firm growth equities kept the move from becoming a defensive liquidation.

## UST Rates, Fed Pricing, Real Yields, And Curve Structure

Treasury losses diminished steadily with maturity. The five-year yield rose five basis points to 4.18%, the 10-year added two basis points to 4.47%, and the 30-year was unchanged at 4.99%. The two-year’s seven-basis-point increase produced a five-basis-point flattening in 2s10s to 42 basis points and a seven-basis-point flattening in 2s30s to 94. The curve located the shock in expected policy and the belly’s exposure to renewed inflation persistence, leaving the far end comparatively anchored after its earlier real-yield adjustment.

Intraday behavior supports that interpretation. Yields had risen by as much as five to nine basis points across maturities when reports of suspended US-Iran exchanges drove US crude sharply higher. Treasuries recovered part of that loss after Washington said Israel and Hezbollah had agreed to halt attacks and talks with Iran were continuing. The incomplete rebound left a clear geopolitical premium in the close, but the long bond’s unchanged finish shows that investors did not simply add the same energy premium to every maturity.

The inflation decomposition was concentrated away from long real rates. The 10-year TIPS yield held at 2.07%, while the 10-year breakeven rose two basis points to 2.4%, accounting for the entire increase in the nominal 10-year yield. Five- and seven-year real yields rose three and two basis points to 1.64% and 1.85%, respectively. Twenty- and 30-year real yields were unchanged at 2.49% and 2.71%. Nearer maturities absorbed both a firmer real-policy path and energy inflation, whereas the long end added no fresh real-rate concession.

Curve curvature conveyed the same maturity split. The 2s5s10s butterfly rose one basis point to minus 16, indicating that the five-year sector underperformed modestly relative to its wings. The 2s10s30s butterfly fell three basis points to minus 10 as the 10-year cheapened against an unchanged long bond but less than the two-year. These were controlled relative-value shifts, not evidence of disorder. The belly remained the meeting point for hike timing, medium-term inflation compensation and uncertainty about how long the energy shock will persist.

Manufacturing releases supplied the domestic component of the selloff. ISM new orders rose to 56.8 from 54.1 and production to 54.3 from 53.4. Employment improved to 48.6 from 46.4 but remained in contraction, while supplier deliveries stayed at 60.6 and prices eased only to 82.1 from 84.6. The final US manufacturing PMI increased to 55.1 from 54.5, with the fastest output growth since April 2022, renewed cost pressure, inventory building and an eleventh month of export weakness. Strong activity and supply friction outweighed the soft labor detail for rates.

Construction spending qualified the growth signal without reversing it. April outlays rose 0.4% to a $2.1724 trillion annualized rate, above a 0.1% pre-release forecast, but the official confidence interval was plus or minus 0.7 percentage point. Private residential construction increased 0.8%, whereas private nonresidential spending fell 0.2% and manufacturing construction declined 1.2% on the month and 18.5% from a year earlier. Rate-sensitive housing retained momentum, but business fixed investment remained uneven; the release supported resilience more than broad acceleration.

Swap pricing translated those inputs into a more immediate tightening path. A hike was fully priced by March 2027, with roughly a 50% chance assigned to a move as early as October. That repricing explains why the two-year and five-year bore the largest losses even though long real yields held steady. The market was not declaring an imminent decision; it was removing confidence that patience would necessarily end in easing when oil, factory demand and pipeline prices were all pressing in the opposite direction.

Bills also moved higher at the policy-sensitive maturities. The 13- and 26-week coupon-equivalent yields each rose three basis points, to 3.71% and 3.78%, while the four- and eight-week tenors added one basis point. Treasury awarded $89 billion of 91-day bills at a 3.630% high discount rate and a 2.69 bid-to-cover ratio, and $77 billion of 182-day bills at 3.665% with 2.84 cover. Those results established completed front-end absorption; the later settlement remains a separate cash event rather than an explanation for the June 1 close.

## Credit, Funding, And Primary-Market Absorption

Corporate financing remained open even as the rate shock weighed on listed credit returns. LQD fell 0.3932%, HYG declined 0.5852% and JNK lost 0.62%. Those instruments combine Treasury duration, carry and credit risk, so the busier primary tape supplied the sharper view of access. High-yield borrowers sold a combined $5.5 billion of notes on the busiest session in five weeks, showing that the selloff raised the discount-rate hurdle without closing the market.

FS KKR Capital delivered the clearest completed absorption case. The business-development company sold $900 million of junk bonds after initially targeting at least $400 million. The notes yielded 7.5%, at the lower end of initial discussion in the mid- to high-7% area, after drawing $1.5 billion of demand. That execution was notable because Moody’s and Fitch had downgraded the company to junk after loan write-downs and dividend cuts, and KKR had recently supplied $300 million of preferred-equity support plus a $150 million share tender.

The deal showed that investors were willing to fund a challenged private-credit vehicle when price and sponsor support compensated for the risk. It did not erase the deterioration in the underlying loan book. Proceeds may refinance debt, and the wider institutional backdrop was constructive: business-development companies had raised more than $14 billion during the year, with issuance exceeding $5.5 billion since that segment reopened in April. Access therefore depended on a visible margin of safety rather than indiscriminate demand for subordinated or opaque credit exposure.

QXO Building Products introduced a larger acquisition-finance test. Banks began marketing $3 billion of junk notes to replace a bridge used for its TopBuild purchase, alongside a $3 billion term loan. The bond portion comprises two $1.5 billion tranches due in 2031 and 2034. Early loan discussions were around 225 basis points over the benchmark at 99 to 99.5, financing part of a roughly $17 billion acquisition. Scarce M&A paper can attract demand, but the package also concentrates cyclical building-products and execution risk in a leveraged capital structure.

AI infrastructure extended the supply channel. Elk Grove Village Property sought $850 million of junk bonds for a Chicago-area data center fully leased to CoreWeave for 15 years, representing about $2.2 billion of revenue. Proceeds would refinance $500 million of existing debt and interest and fund remaining construction. Companies tied to the AI buildout had already raised nearly $27 billion in riskier bonds during the year. Long contracted revenue improves visibility, yet construction completion, tenant concentration and refinancing still determine how much spread investors require.

Loan markets revealed demand through repricing and maturity extension. European borrowers including Restaurant Brands Europe, nexeye and Parts Holding Europe sought cuts of 25 to 50 basis points on existing euro loans as year-to-date issuance lagged the prior year by about 8% at $124.8 billion equivalent. Citadel Securities marketed a $4.25 billion loan due in 2033 at an initial 200-basis-point spread and 99.5 to 99.75, extending a $3.95 billion facility due in 2031 and adding $300 million. Scarcity supported lenders’ willingness to roll exposure, though repeated compression reduced their compensation.

Atalian showed the other end of credit differentiation. Holders of about 60% of the French facilities-management group’s €919 million of notes due in 2028 agreed to take control through a restructuring. Roughly two-thirds of liabilities would convert into majority equity, the remaining debt would extend to 2031, and investors committed up to €140 million of new super-senior financing. Primary access was therefore broad but not universal: healthy or well-supported borrowers refinanced and upsized, whereas impaired balance sheets required equitization, maturity relief and senior new money.

## Global Duration, Central Banks, And Fiscal Transmission

European rates supplied a hawkish global backdrop to the US bear flattening. ECB Executive Board member Isabel Schnabel argued that the Iran-war shock could no longer be treated as a temporary energy disturbance because damage to infrastructure and supply chains was spreading pressure into goods prices and expectations. Her support for a rate increase was paired with caution about the path beyond the first move. That stance raises global front-end discount rates and makes it harder for Treasuries to sustain an independent easing narrative when US factory prices are also elevated.

Euro-area manufacturing complicated the policy choice. The final PMI fell to 51.6 in May from 52.2, and the output index slowed to 51.3 from 52.3. New orders stagnated, exports declined and employment contracted, yet input costs rose at the fastest pace since May 2022 and output-charge inflation reached a three-and-a-half-year high. Weaker activity would ordinarily support duration; faster pipeline inflation points toward tighter policy. The resulting stagflation mix transmits volatility to US rates through relative policy pricing and global term-premium competition.

Consumer expectations offered some restraint on the most hawkish interpretation. Euro-area three-year inflation expectations eased to 2.9% in April from 3%, while one-year expectations stayed at 4% and the five-year measure held at 2.4%, still above the ECB’s target. The slight medium-horizon improvement did not neutralize persistent short- and long-horizon concern. It instead left policymakers balancing a slowing manufacturing sector against the risk that energy and supply-chain costs become embedded, a balance similar to the one confronting the Fed.

Japan added a currency and buyer-base channel. Record intervention had not overcome the wide US-Japan rate gap, and overnight swaps assigned about a 78% probability to a near-term BOJ increase. Elevated oil raised Japan’s import bill and inflation pressure while yen weakness reduced the durability of intervention gains. A more hawkish BOJ can raise domestic yields and reduce the relative attraction of hedged foreign bonds, requiring US and European duration to clear with less dependable Japanese demand.

South Korea used supply management to contain the same pressure. Finance-ministry officials had conducted daily calls with dealers and asset managers since May 18, and the government reduced planned June bond issuance by about 21% after the 10-year yield reached its highest level since late 2023. Lower supply can moderate a disorderly rise, but it cannot remove imported inflation or a more hawkish central-bank stance. The policy response therefore relieved local duration pressure at the margin without changing the global energy mechanism.

After the New York cutoff, South Korea’s May consumer inflation accelerated to 3.1% from 2.6%, above a 2.9% median estimate, and core inflation reached 2.5%. That release did not cause the completed Treasury move. It did reinforce the next-session risk that fuel and travel costs were broadening beyond headline energy, supporting the Bank of Korea’s hawkish tilt. China provided a counterweight earlier in the window: its manufacturing PMI eased to 51.8 from 52.2 as exports weakened and input- and output-price inflation slowed.

## EM Sovereigns, Dollar Liquidity, And Debt-Management Risk

Emerging-market dollar debt faced a less favorable combination of US rates, crude and the dollar. EMB fell 0.4355%, and the dollar index gained 0.2932%, tightening the broad external backdrop at the same time as higher oil worsened import costs for energy-dependent sovereigns. The listed-fund move describes total-return pressure rather than a uniform cash-spread change. Country outcomes depended on how the energy bill interacted with reserves, fiscal subsidies, local policy credibility and debt terms.

India sat closest to that adverse intersection. Its 10-year sovereign yield had risen about 34 basis points to 7% since the Iran war began, with five-year bonds bearing an even larger increase as markets priced monetary tightening. Heavy oil imports, a weaker rupee and fuel and fertilizer subsidies linked the external shock directly to inflation and public borrowing. Kotak Mahindra estimated that revenue shortfalls and higher subsidies could widen the fiscal deficit to 4.6% of GDP from a 4.3% budget target, with 4.8% possible in a more adverse case.

The Indian curve was pricing more than a simple imported-price spike. Some managers expected 75 to 100 basis points of increases during the cycle, and the five-year swap had risen more than 60 basis points since the conflict started. Other investors argued that supply-led inflation should make the central bank cautious about tightening into weaker demand. That disagreement leaves short and intermediate sovereign bonds sensitive to both oil persistence and rupee intervention, while the fiscal channel raises the compensation required farther along the government borrowing program.

Chile illustrated how quickly the same geopolitical factor could reverse local relative value. Earlier optimism about an agreement had pulled long-term breakevens and sovereign risk measures back toward pre-war levels and shifted investor interest toward short-dated nominal peso bonds. The share favoring one- to five-year inflation-linked instruments fell to almost 54% from 72% a month earlier. The later suspension headlines and oil rebound challenged that normalization, reopening the question of how rapidly higher pump prices pass through to consumer inflation.

Zambia’s dispute was driven by contract design rather than broad EM beta. Holders of more than 25% of its $1.36 billion 2053 dollar bond opposed a tender they considered adverse. The government offered $780 per $1,000 for early participation and $740 later; acceptance by 75% could activate a clean-up call and remove remaining investors before a possible coupon step-up from 0.5% to 7.5%. The conflict concentrates risk in collective action, recovery value and debt-sustainability conditions even when the broader dollar backdrop is manageable.

Debt swaps offered a slower refinancing channel for developing sovereigns facing high yields. Enosis sought to expand transactions that refinance government debt on better terms and direct savings toward environmental or social projects, supported by a $1 billion five-year commitment from Legal & General. More than $1 trillion of debt may be eligible, yet only about $6 billion of private-capital deals had been completed since 2021. The gap shows both the potential relief and the execution limits of structured debt management when conventional refinancing becomes expensive.

## Cross asset

Crude supplied the clearest confirmation of the rates thesis. WTI rose 5.4945% to $92.16 and Brent gained 3.1831% to $94.98 as stalled negotiation headlines revived concern about Middle East supply. The larger WTI move aligned with the two-basis-point increase in 10-year breakevens and the front-end selloff. Later diplomatic reassurance reduced the intraday extremes but did not restore the prior close’s energy-relief configuration, leaving oil as the session’s dominant inflation transmission channel.

Equities resisted the bond-market tightening. The S&P 500 advanced 0.2625%, the Nasdaq 100 rose 0.5957% and the semiconductor index gained 1.0622%. AI earnings and investment expectations supported growth shares even as higher real rates pressured shorter Treasury maturities. That coexistence fits resilient nominal activity rather than recession fear. It also contains a fixed-income counterforce: large technology companies’ planned capital spending of as much as $725 billion can support future productivity, but initially increases demand for chips, power, construction and debt financing.

Volatility and the dollar registered more concern than equities. The VIX rose 4.765% to 16.05, and the dollar index gained 0.2932% to 99.2 as higher US yields and geopolitical demand reinforced one another. Leveraged funds, asset managers and other speculative accounts held about $16.5 billion of long-dollar wagers, up from roughly $5 billion three weeks earlier. A firmer dollar compounds the oil shock for import-dependent sovereigns and tightens funding conditions outside the United States.

Gold fell 1.8704% to 4475.2002 despite the renewed geopolitical tension. The decline, together with rising equities, indicates that the session was not a uniform haven rush. Investors rewarded AI-linked growth, marked up conventional dollar and policy-rate exposure, and reduced both government-bond and gold prices. Credit gauges weakened more than equities, preserving an unresolved question about whether abundant primary-market capital can continue offsetting a higher all-in cost of funds if crude remains elevated.

## Forward Market Significance

The June 2 JOLTS release, June 3 ISM services survey, June 4 claims and revised productivity figures, and June 5 employment report form the next test of the front-end tightening repricing. The observable triggers are whether openings, hiring and quits remain firm; whether services employment and prices echo the factory surveys; and whether payrolls, unemployment, earnings and unit labor costs indicate persistent demand pressure. Resilient labor demand paired with elevated services prices would confirm the June 1 bear flattening and keep the two- and five-year sectors sensitive to an earlier Fed increase. Broad cooling in hiring and prices would challenge the manufacturing-led interpretation, lower real-policy pressure and resolve some of the tension between contracting factory employment and stronger output.

The condition of US-Iran negotiations and Strait of Hormuz flows, followed by the RBI decision on June 5, the ECB meeting on June 10-11 and the BOJ decision on June 16, will test whether the energy shock is becoming an internationally synchronized policy impulse. The observable triggers are a durable restoration of oil exports, sustained crude declines, firmer local currencies and central-bank guidance that separates temporary energy effects from broader inflation. Secure flows and patient guidance would challenge the current breakeven rise and reopen the prior energy-relief interpretation. Continued disruption, renewed crude gains or coordinated hawkish signals would confirm the stagflation channel, with US breakevens, European and Asian front ends, the dollar and oil-importing sovereign curves most sensitive.

Treasury’s long-duration buyback on June 3, the front-end cash-management buyback and coupon-supply announcement on June 4, and execution of QXO’s loan and bond package over June 2-3 will test whether intermediation can absorb public and private supply at the higher rate structure. Stable bill yields, orderly settlement, strong QXO orders and pricing near initial discussion would confirm that the June 1 selloff reflected macro repricing rather than a funding constraint. A weak reception, a large corporate concession or renewed long-bond underperformance around the announced 3-, 10- and 30-year supply would challenge that conclusion. The sensitive channels are repo and bill rates, the Treasury belly and long end, high-yield new-issue concessions and the broader refinancing pipeline.

section_summaries.market_read: The close was a front-end policy repricing with resilient risk absorption, not a duration shock.
section_summaries.next_check: Oil persistence and incoming labor data will determine whether the flattening can hold.
section_summaries.summary: Renewed US-Iran uncertainty lifted crude and revived inflation pass-through risk, while firm manufacturing deepened front-end Treasury losses. The resulting bear flattening expressed a more restrictive Fed path rather than a broad duration retreat, as open primary markets and advancing growth equities restrained defensive pressure.

section_summaries.market_read: Rates located the shock in Fed timing and medium-term inflation, leaving term premium contained.
section_summaries.next_check: Services prices and labor resilience are the next test of front-end tightening expectations.
section_summaries.section_title: UST Rates, Fed Pricing, Real Yields, And Curve Structure
section_summaries.summary: Treasury losses faded with maturity as the two- and five-year sectors absorbed higher policy and inflation risk, while the long bond and long real yields stayed anchored. Rising breakevens explained the nominal 10-year move, and modest butterfly shifts showed controlled rather than disorderly repricing.

section_summaries.market_read: Primary-market execution suggests a higher discount-rate hurdle, not a systemic funding break.
section_summaries.next_check: QXO order quality and concessions will show whether leveraged supply can clear cleanly.
section_summaries.summary: Credit return gauges weakened with higher Treasury yields, but financing access remained open as high-yield supply was absorbed and a downgraded borrower upsized at favorable terms. Differentiation still mattered: supported issuers refinanced, while impaired balance sheets required equitization, maturity relief and senior capital.

section_summaries.market_read: Global policy repricing reduces room for an independent US easing narrative.
section_summaries.next_check: ECB, BOJ and RBI guidance must separate temporary energy pressure from persistent inflation.
section_summaries.section_title: Global Duration, Central Banks, And Fiscal Transmission
section_summaries.summary: European pipeline inflation, hawkish central-bank signals and Japan’s buyer-base dynamics reinforced the global front-end pressure facing Treasuries. Slower euro-area activity and managed Korean bond supply tempered duration stress, but neither removed the energy shock’s inflation or cross-market policy transmission.

section_summaries.market_read: EM pressure is differentiated, but oil-importing sovereigns face the tightest external mix.
section_summaries.next_check: Watch crude persistence, local-currency stability and policy responses in major oil importers.
section_summaries.section_title: EM Sovereigns, Dollar Liquidity, And Debt-Management Risk
section_summaries.summary: Higher US rates, crude and the dollar tightened conditions for emerging-market debt, with oil importers exposed through inflation, currencies and fiscal subsidies. India faced the clearest policy conflict, while Zambia’s tender dispute and slow debt-swap execution highlighted idiosyncratic refinancing and contract risks.

section_summaries.market_read: Cross-asset pricing reflected resilient nominal growth alongside renewed inflation risk.
section_summaries.next_check: Sustained crude gains with weaker risk assets would signal a more defensive transmission.
section_summaries.summary: Oil confirmed the inflation impulse as equities advanced on AI-linked growth, while higher volatility and a firmer dollar showed broader caution. Gold and bonds both fell, arguing against a uniform haven move and leaving credit absorption as the key counterweight to higher funding costs.

section_summaries.market_read: Confirmation requires persistent demand and inflation without deterioration in market absorption.
section_summaries.next_check: Track the three defined tests through June 16 for convergence or a clear thesis break.
section_summaries.summary: Three tests will determine whether the selloff remains macro repricing or becomes a broader funding problem: US labor and services data, energy flows with central-bank guidance, and Treasury and corporate supply absorption. Each channel bears directly on front-end policy pricing, global inflation transmission or intermediation capacity.

methodology_note: Market levels are shown on an NY-close basis unless otherwise noted.

bullets: 10Y TIPS real yield 2.07% (0 bp) | 10Y breakeven inflation 2.4% (+2 bp)
UST 2s10s curve 42 bp (-5 bp) | UST 2s30s curve 94 bp (-7 bp)
overall_summary.headline: Treasuries bear-flatten as oil and factory prices revive Fed tightening risk
overall_summary.session_narrative: The NY close delivered a controlled bear flattening as renewed US-Iran uncertainty lifted crude and strong manufacturing restored inflation and Fed-tightening risk. The shock passed through policy-sensitive and intermediate Treasuries, while the long bond and long real yields remained comparatively anchored. Open financing markets and advancing growth equities limited the move from becoming a generalized flight from duration or risk.
overall_summary.summary: Front-end Treasury yields led losses as renewed oil-supply anxiety and firm US manufacturing reopened inflation and Fed risks. Long real yields stayed anchored, while functioning primary markets and firmer growth equities limited the move from becoming a broad defensive liquidation.

watch_events.market_implication: Confirmation would keep two- and five-year Treasuries exposed to further policy repricing.
watch_events.market_trigger: Firm hiring with elevated services prices would validate an earlier Fed increase.
watch_events.title: US labor and services test
watch_events.watch_line: Track job openings, services prices, claims, payrolls, earnings and unit labor costs.

watch_events.market_implication: Breakevens, global front ends, the dollar and oil-importer curves would face renewed pressure.
watch_events.market_trigger: Continued disruption, renewed crude gains or coordinated hawkish guidance would confirm persistence.
watch_events.title: Energy flows and central-bank response
watch_events.watch_line: Watch US-Iran talks, Strait flows, crude, currencies and RBI, ECB and BOJ guidance.

watch_events.market_implication: Weak reception or larger concessions would pressure Treasury duration and refinancing pipelines.
watch_events.market_trigger: Orderly settlement and strong demand near initial terms would confirm intact intermediation.
watch_events.title: Public and private supply absorption
watch_events.watch_line: Follow Treasury buybacks and coupon plans alongside execution of QXO’s financing package.
